summary: Renamed the old address-based menu unit mn_2295 into mnmain, split the later rule-related address range into a new mnmainrule compilation unit, and added a partial C implementation of mn_80231804. The PR updated GALE01 splits, symbols, configure.py object lists, includes across menu/game/user code, and introduced mnmainrule.h for the extracted functions. decomp.dev reported the apparent 32 new/32 broken matches as rename churn from mn_2295 to mnmain, plus mnmainrule::mn_80231804 improving from 0.00% to 15.15%. No human review text was present in the slice.
what_changed: Renamed src/melee/mn/mn_2295.c to src/melee/mn/mnmain.c and src/melee/mn/mn_2295.h to src/melee/mn/mnmain.h.

Split the former mn_2295 range in config/GALE01/splits.txt: mnmain now covers text 0x802295AC-0x8022FEC8 and mnmainrule covers text 0x8022FEC8-0x8023201C, with corresponding data/sdata/sdata2 boundaries.
Added src/melee/mn/mnmainrule.c containing placeholders for functions starting at mn_8022FEC8, the existing matched helper mn_80231634, and a partial implementation of mn_80231804.
Added src/melee/mn/mnmainrule.h with prototypes for the extracted rule-range functions and moved struct mn_80231634_t out of mnmain.h into mnmainrule.h.
Updated configure.py to build both Object(NonMatching, "melee/mn/mnmain.c") and Object(NonMatching, "melee/mn/mnmainrule.c") instead of the single old mn_2295 object.
Updated includes in gm and mn users from mn_2295.h to mnmain.h where they depend on the main-menu declarations.
Changed mnmain.h guard from an address-style GALE01_2295AC name to MELEE_MN_MAIN_H and typed fn_8022F538 as void fn_8022F538(HSD_GObj*) instead of UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS.
Split a large local data symbol mn_803EC864 of size 0x96C into many individual lbl_803ECxxx data:string symbols in config/GALE01/symbols.txt.

mn_80231804 now initializes menu globals, calls HSD_SisLib_803A5E70, loads many MenMain archive sections with lbArchive_LoadSections, sets mn_804D6BD4, invokes existing menu setup functions, creates a GObj process for fn_8022F538, calls mn_80230E38(1), and starts audio via lbAudioAx_80023F28(gmMainLib_8015ECB0()).
smart_moves: The PR separated a semantic submodule, mnmainrule, at an exact section boundary rather than keeping unrelated late functions in the renamed mnmain file.
The split was made consistently across source files, headers, splits.txt, configure.py, and symbol metadata, which is necessary for decomp build accounting and match reports to remain meaningful.
Using descriptive file names mnmain and mnmainrule replaced the address-derived mn_2295 name, making future work easier to search and organize.

The large contiguous data blob was decomposed into individual string labels, which gives the decompiler concrete data boundaries needed for archive-section string references and cleaner future reconstruction.

The new mn_80231804 implementation uses explicit archive asset names and StaticModelDesc fields, turning a previously opaque placeholder into a readable sequence of menu resource loads even though it is only a partial match.
The header split kept the rule-specific struct and prototypes near their implementation, reducing mnmain.h exposure while preserving dependent declarations in mnmain.h for existing users.
decomp_lessons: When splitting an address-named C unit, update all four coupling points together: source/header rename, include users, configure.py object list, and config/GALE01/splits.txt section ranges.
A file rename can appear in decomp.dev as equal numbers of new and broken matches; inspect the unit names before treating those as real regressions.
For a new submodule, carve the .text/.data/.sdata/.sdata2 ranges on observed boundaries so subsequent matching work can be attributed to the correct object.
Moving prototypes out of a broad header into a submodule header is useful only if all external include users still get the declarations they actually need.
Typing callbacks and APIs incrementally, such as changing fn_8022F538 from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to void fn_8022F538(HSD_GObj*), can help later callsites and proc-creation matches.
Archive-loading functions often need exact string-symbol granularity; replacing a single large data object with many data:string labels can unblock partial source for resource-load tables.
naming_conventions: Prefer semantic menu file names such as mnmain.c and mnmainrule.c over address-derived names such as mn_2295.c when module identity is known.
Use project-style header guards for named modules: MELEE_MN_MAIN_H and MELEE_MN_MAINRULE_H.
Keep unresolved address-based function names, such as mn_80231804 and fn_802309F0, until better semantic names are proven.
Use lbl_803ECxxx for local string/data labels when the symbol identity is not yet semantically known.
StaticModelDesc externs in this slice retain asset-derived names like MenMainBack_Top, MenMainPanel_Top, MenMainCursorRl01_Top, and MenMainCursorSs_Top.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Object splitting was done by moving placeholders and the small matched helper mn_80231634 from the end of mnmain.c into a new mnmainrule.c at the exact 0x8022FEC8 boundary.
Partial matching of mn_80231804 was attempted by expressing the large lbArchive_LoadSections vararg call directly with ordered pairs of destination pointers and archive section names.
The implementation preserves visible initialization order for globals mn_804D6BC8 and mn_804A04F0 before library calls, which is often critical for matching stores.
The code stores the result of HSD_GObjProc_8038FD54 in a temporary HSD_GObjProc* and then assigns temp_r3->flags_3 = HSD_GObj_804D783C, exposing an ordering-sensitive proc creation pattern.
decomp.dev reported mnmainrule::mn_80231804 improved by 290 bytes from 0.00% to 15.15%, while renamed mnmain functions retained their previous match coverage under a new unit name.
review_feedback: No human review comments or PR body were present in the slice.
Automated decomp.dev report showed 32 new matches in main/melee/mn/mnmain and 32 broken matches in main/melee/mn/mn_2295, consistent with the file rename from mn_2295 to mnmain.
Automated decomp.dev report listed 13 improvements in unmatched functions, including mnmainrule::mn_80231804 at 15.15% after the PR.
Automated decomp.dev report listed 12 regressions in unmatched functions under the old mn_2295 unit name, which appears to be rename accounting rather than evidence of source quality regression.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that continue matching mn_80231804 in src/melee/mn/mnmainrule.c.
Search for future semantic names for mnmainrule functions mn_8022FEC8 through mn_802339FC.
Check whether MenMainCursorRl_Top being loaded twice in mn_80231804 remained intentional or was later corrected.
Look for later replacements of lbl_803EC864-style string labels with semantic names.
Compare decomp.dev reports after this PR to confirm renamed mnmain matches were recognized as stable rather than real regressions.
